inputs.userGoal: Deploy a real-time recommendation engine as an Azure ML managed online endpoint, scale for high request volumes, and store usage activity in Cosmos DB.

architecturePrinciples: Horizontally scalable for high request volumes
Managed services to minimize operational overhead
Secure by default with system-assigned managed identity
Observable with centralized logging and monitoring

reasoning.whyChosen: Required backing store for workspace-based Application Insights. Centralizes logs from ML workspace and online endpoint for operational visibility (WAF: Operational Excellence).
reasoning.tradeoffs: PerGB2018 pay-as-you-go is cost-efficient for moderate log volumes; switch to CapacityReservation if ingestion exceeds 100 GB/day.

resources.name: appi-recommender-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Provides request/response telemetry, latency tracking, and failure diagnostics for the ML online endpoint (WAF: Operational Excellence). Workspace-based mode is required by the ML workspace pairing constraint.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Full telemetry increases log ingestion cost but is essential for monitoring real-time inference latency and errors.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Workspace-based Application Insights requires a Log Analytics workspace as its backing store.

reasoning.whyChosen: Required linked storage for the ML workspace — stores experiment data, model artifacts, and logs (WAF: Security, Reliability). StorageV2 is mandated by the ML workspace pairing constraint.
alternativesConsidered: Premium block blob storage (unnecessary for ML workspace metadata)
reasoning.tradeoffs: Standard_LRS is cost-effective; upgrade to Standard_ZRS for zone redundancy if required.

reasoning.whyChosen: Required linked Key Vault for the ML workspace — secures secrets, connection strings, and encryption keys (WAF: Security). Soft delete is mandated by the ML workspace pairing constraint.
alternativesConsidered: Premium tier with HSM-protected keys (unnecessary unless regulatory requirement)
reasoning.tradeoffs: Standard tier covers software-protected keys; upgrade to Premium only for HSM compliance.

reasoning.whyChosen: Stores custom Docker images for the ML managed online endpoint scoring environment (WAF: Operational Excellence). Required when deploying custom inference containers.
alternativesConsidered: Premium ACR with geo-replication (overkill for single-region deployment)
Standard ACR (100 GiB storage, more than needed initially)
reasoning.tradeoffs: Basic tier provides 10 GiB storage sufficient for initial model images; upgrade to Standard/Premium as image count grows.

resources.name: mlw-recommender-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Central workspace for model management, experiment tracking, and hosting managed online endpoints for real-time inference (WAF: Operational Excellence, Performance Efficiency). Managed online endpoints provide auto-scaling, blue/green deployments, and built-in load balancing.
alternativesConsidered: Azure Functions (cold-start latency unsuitable for real-time)
reasoning.tradeoffs: Managed online endpoints abstract cluster management but have less customization than self-hosted AKS; suitable for most real-time ML workloads.

resources.dependencyReasoning: ML workspace requires linked Storage Account and Key Vault (immutable after creation). Application Insights and Container Registry are recommended for monitoring and custom environment support.

resources.name: mlw-recommender-prod-001/recommender-endpoint

reasoning.whyChosen: Managed online endpoint provides a scalable HTTPS inference URL with built-in autoscaling, load balancing, and blue/green deployment support for the recommendation model (WAF: Performance Efficiency, Reliability).
reasoning.tradeoffs: Key-based auth is simpler to start; switch to AAD token auth for production zero-trust scenarios.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Online endpoint is a child resource of the ML workspace.

references.title: Deploy models with managed online endpoints

reasoning.whyChosen: Cosmos DB NoSQL API provides single-digit millisecond read/write latency ideal for recording user activity events at high throughput (WAF: Performance Efficiency, Reliability). Session consistency balances performance and read-your-writes guarantee for activity tracking.
alternativesConsidered: Azure Table Storage (lower cost but limited querying)
Azure SQL Database (higher latency for high-volume writes)
Event Hubs + downstream store (adds architectural complexity)
reasoning.tradeoffs: Cosmos DB is premium-priced compared to Table Storage; justified by sub-10ms latency, global distribution option, and rich querying. Provisioned throughput (RU/s) should be configured on the container to match expected write volume — start with autoscale 400-4000 RU/s.

resources.name: cosmos-recommender-prod-001/activity-db

reasoning.whyChosen: Logical database to group usage activity containers within the Cosmos DB account.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Single database is sufficient; add shared throughput if multiple containers need cost sharing.

resources.dependencyReasoning: SQL database is a child resource of the Cosmos DB account.

references.title: Cosmos DB SQL API databases and containers

resources.name: cosmos-recommender-prod-001/activity-db/user-activity

reasoning.whyChosen: Container to store user interaction/activity events partitioned by userId for efficient per-user queries and even data distribution (WAF: Performance Efficiency). Autoscale 400–4000 RU/s handles variable traffic without manual tuning.
alternativesConsidered: Fixed throughput (risk of throttling during spikes)
reasoning.tradeoffs: Autoscale costs ~1.5× the minimum RU rate but prevents 429 throttling under burst traffic.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Container is a child resource of the SQL database.

overallReasoning.summary: This architecture uses Azure ML managed online endpoints for scalable, low-latency real-time inference and Cosmos DB NoSQL API for high-throughput usage activity storage. Supporting resources (Storage, Key Vault, ACR, App Insights, Log Analytics) fulfill ML workspace requirements and provide full observability. All resources deploy to a single region (eastus) with managed identities for secure service-to-service communication.
overallReasoning.tradeoffs: Single-region deployment simplifies cost and latency but lacks geo-redundancy. Cosmos DB autoscale adds a cost premium over fixed throughput. Managed online endpoints trade customization for operational simplicity versus self-managed AKS inference. ACR Basic tier may need upgrading as model image count grows.

plan.validation: All ML workspace pairing constraints satisfied: StorageV2 storage account, Key Vault with soft-delete, workspace-based Application Insights backed by Log Analytics. Cosmos DB uses Session consistency (no conflict with single-region setup). No subnet or network conflicts — all resources use public endpoints by default. Container Registry linked to workspace for custom scoring images.
